relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul answers a Corinthian claim about abstaining from touch.
Marriage is offered as embodied faithfulness amid sexual misuse.
Mutual care is owed in marriage.
Authority over the body is mutual, not one-sided domination.
Abstinence requires agreement, prayer, and return, not coercion.
This is concession, not command.
Different life-states are different gifts.
Singleness can be beautiful and whole.
Marriage is better than burning with unmet desire.
Marriage is not to be casually severed.
Separation seeks either singleness or reconciliation.
Mixed marriage may remain if peaceable.
A trusting wife is not told to abandon a willing untrusting husband.
Household holiness can flow through the trusting partner.
If the untrusting one leaves, peace matters; the sibling is not enslaved.
No spouse can control whether the other will be saved/healed.
Walk in the condition of calling assigned by the Lord.
Circumcision status need not be manipulated.
External marker is not the center; Divine command-keeping is.
Remain with the Divine inside your calling.
Enslaved status need not define the soul; freedom may be used when possible.
The enslaved in the Lord is freed; the free belongs to Messiah.
Bought with price, do not become enslaved to humans.
Remain with the Divine where you were called.
Paul gives trustworthy judgment, not command, about unmarried ones.
Present distress shapes the counsel.
Do not anxiously force a change of state.
Marriage is not misalignment, though it carries pressure.
The shortened season loosens ordinary possession.
Grief, joy, and buying are relativized by the passing form.
The world’s present form is passing away.
Paul desires freedom from anxiety.
Marriage brings relational concerns into devotion.
Unmarried and married callings divide attention differently.
The aim is benefit and undistracted devotion, not a noose.
Marriage remains permitted when desire and timing require it.
Firm discernment may also preserve singleness.
Both choices can be beautiful, with Paul preferring less distraction.
Widowhood opens freedom to remarry in the Lord.
Paul offers his judgment toward flourishing and Spirit-discernment.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 7 is not control-code; it is pastoral triage under pressure. Mutuality, peace, freedom from coercion, and devotion are the thread.
